finding: Primary human melanocytes express GPER (membrane estrogen receptor) but LACK classical ERα/ERβ; 17β-estradiol raises melanin ~208% via GPER→cAMP→pCREB→MITF; GPER knockout abolishes it. The melasma/pregnancy mechanism.
Estrogen via GPER (GPR30) raises intracellular cAMP — the same second messenger as the MC1R→PKA→CREB→MITF axis.
ERβ (ESR2) protein detected by IHC in ~90% of epidermal melanocytes in healthy skin margins.
AR expressed (nuclear IHC + mRNA) in cultured human GENITAL melanocytes; androgen (T→DHT) increases tyrosinase activity, blocked by AR antagonist. Regional, not general-epidermal.
Human skin locally synthesizes sex steroids (aromatase in sebaceous/sweat/dermal-papilla cells) — a paracrine estrogen source; melanocyte-intrinsic aromatase NOT shown.
Cultured melanocytes stain for generic 'ER' but ERα identity not distinguished; multiple later IHC studies find normal melanocytes ERα-NEGATIVE. ESR1 role is contested/weak.
